rationale: In the citing paper, Han et al. (2019) is explicitly used to justify selecting relative firm performance as an organizational moderator because prior work shows managerial risk taking depends on relative performance. That citation feeds into hypothesis development (H3) and the conceptual setup of Study 1, not just a generic literature list. Although the citing paper does not reuse Han et al.'s SDO mechanism or specific empirical design, it meaningfully transfers a core contingency insight into the CMO-gender context.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Han et al. (2019)'s finding that risk-taking behavior is contingent on relative performance to motivate and test a moderator in female vs. male CMO marketing decisions. The cited work is used as conceptual support for this contingency argument rather than as a direct methodological template.
